channel.title: Void Linux on My Linux Journey
channel.description: Recent content in Void Linux on My Linux Journey

item.title: How to Setup and Configure Wi-Fi on Void Linux (Step-by-Step)
item.author: youcef7770@gmail.com (Youcef)

item.title: How to Install Docker on Void Linux Step by Step
item.author: youcef7770@gmail.com (Youcef)
item.description: How to Install Docker on Void Linux

item.title: Fix: modprobe FATAL Module dm_mod Not Found in Void Linux
item.author: youcef7770@gmail.com (Youcef)
item.description: Encountering ‘modprobe: FATAL: Module dm_mod not found in directory /lib/modules/…’ on Void Linux? This guide walks you through chrooting from a live ISO and rebuilding your initramfs and kernel hooks with xbps-reconfigure.

item.title: How to Reinstall initramfs, vmlinuz, and Kernel on Void Linux
item.author: youcef7770@gmail.com (Youcef)
item.description: Learn step-by-step how to recover your Void Linux system when initramfs, vmlinuz, or kernel files are deleted, by booting from a live USB and using xbps-install with dracut.

item.title: Setup KVM + QEMU + libvirt on Void Linux
item.author: youcef7770@gmail.com (Youcef)
item.description: Complete guide to setting up KVM + QEMU + libvirt on Void Linux. Covers hardware virtualization check, package installation, libvirt configuration, and VM management.

item.title: Clean XBPS Cache on Void Linux - Free Disk Space
item.author: youcef7770@gmail.com (Youcef)
item.description: Guide to cleaning Void Linux package cache using xbps-remove commands. Covers removing all cached packages, obsolete packages, and manual cleanup.

item.title: Install CUPS on Void Linux - Printer Setup Guide
item.author: youcef7770@gmail.com (Youcef)
item.description: Complete guide to installing CUPS on Void Linux. Covers installation via XBPS, runit service configuration, web interface access, and printer management.

item.title: Install Sway on Void Linux - Complete Guide
item.author: youcef7770@gmail.com (Youcef)
item.description: Step-by-step guide to install Sway on Void Linux. Includes seatd setup, greetd display manager, waybar configuration, and troubleshooting.

item.title: Install Wayland Compositors on Void Linux
item.author: youcef7770@gmail.com (Youcef)
item.description: Complete guide to installing Wayland compositors on Void Linux. Covers Sway, labwc, wayfire, seatd, and display manager setup.

item.title: Install XFCE on Void Linux - All Methods Guide
item.author: youcef7770@gmail.com (Youcef)
item.description: Comprehensive guide to installing XFCE on Void Linux. Covers meta-package, minimal components, display managers, startx, XWayland, VNC, and Docker methods.

item.title: List Installed Packages on Void Linux - Complete Guide
item.author: youcef7770@gmail.com (Youcef)
item.description: Guide to listing installed packages on Void Linux. Uses xbps-query to show all packages, manually installed programs, versions, and orphaned dependencies.

item.title: Sound Setup on Void Linux
item.author: youcef7770@gmail.com (Youcef)
item.description: Guide to configuring audio on Void Linux. Covers ALSA, PulseAudio, PipeWire setup, alsamixer, and troubleshooting sound issues.

item.title: Install Local XBPS Packages on Void Linux
item.author: youcef7770@gmail.com (Youcef)
item.description: Guide to installing downloaded XBPS packages on Void Linux manually. Uses xbps-rindex to index packages and xbps-install to install locally.

item.title: Runit Service Management on Void Linux
item.author: youcef7770@gmail.com (Youcef)
item.description: Complete guide to Runit service management on Void Linux. Covers sv up, down, restart, status, and service enabling/disabling.

item.title: Install DWM and ST on Void Linux - Suckless Tools
item.author: youcef7770@gmail.com (Youcef)
item.description: Guide to installing DWM (Dynamic Window Manager) and ST terminal on Void Linux. Covers build from source, patches, and configuration.

item.title: Linux Kernel Insights - What I Learned
item.author: youcef7770@gmail.com (Youcef)
item.description: Collection of insights about the Linux kernel. Covers checking kernel version, system calls, kernel modules, and core Linux kernel concepts.
